data.title: ViaBTC Launches 10th Anniversary Campaign 'Proof of Decade'
data.description: ViaBTC Group, a leading global blockchain infrastructure provider, has announced the launch of its 10th-anniversary campaign, "Proof of Decade," marking a significant milestone since its founding in 2016. According to BeInCrypto, the campaign celebrates a decade of continuous development and reliable service to miners, traders, and crypto users worldwide. ViaBTC Pool, renowned for its top ranking in LTC/DOGE merged mining, ZEC mining, and BCH mining, supports over ten major PoW cryptocurrencies. The campaign includes community celebrations and a $100,000 prize pool initiative starting June 2, 2026.

data.title: CertiK CEO Warns of Risks from Unisolated AI Agents
data.description: CertiK CEO Ronghui Gu has issued a warning regarding the potential risks posed by unisolated AI agents. According to NS3.AI, Gu highlighted that these agents could lead to the exposure of sensitive files, credentials, and financial accounts. CertiK's investigations have uncovered numerous malicious skills, fake installers, and deceptive dependency packages on open agent utility hubs. This discovery underscores the importance of ensuring robust security measures in AI deployments to protect against such vulnerabilities.

data.title: Gold Surpasses US Dollar in Central Bank Reserves, Fidelity Reports
data.description: Fidelity Digital Assets has reported that gold has surpassed U.S. dollar assets in global central bank reserves following a significant decline of approximately 20% from around $5,600 per ounce. According to NS3.AI, Fidelity noted that Bitcoin is increasingly being used in cross-border trade settlements, indicating a move away from dollar-based systems. However, the anticipated outperformance of Bitcoin compared to gold has not yet been realized, the firm added.

data.title: Trump's Iran Deal Remarks Boost Markets, Oil Drops
data.description: President Trump announced on Truth Social that he is meeting in the 'Situation Room' to make a final determination on the Iran peace deal, and that the naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will be lifted. This news led to a more than 2% drop in WTI crude oil prices, now below $87 per barrel, according to CoinDesk. U.S. stocks, which were previously flat, are now showing modest gains, with the Nasdaq up 0.2%. Bitcoin (BTC) has also risen about $1,000 from the morning's lows, reaching $73,500.

data.title: Polymarket Partners with OneFootball to Launch Exclusive Football Prediction Market
data.description: Polymarket has announced an exclusive partnership with sports media platform OneFootball. According to Foresight News, this collaboration will allow OneFootball users to access Polymarket's football prediction market.

data.title: Brookings Fellow Warns of CFTC's Resource Needs Amid Crypto Legislation
data.description: Aaron Klein, a Brookings Institution fellow, has raised concerns about the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) ability to oversee digital markets as Congress considers expanding its authority over crypto assets. According to CoinDesk, Klein emphasized that the CFTC, originally designed for commodity futures, lacks the resources, independence, and coordination needed for its expanded role. He warned that without additional staff and funding, the agency might appear to regulate without effective oversight, risking past regulatory failures. Klein also criticized political influence in financial regulation.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Continued Downward Pressure Amid Key Support Levels
data.description:
Ethereum has continued its downward trend after falling below the critical $2,000 support level. According to Odaily, market analysts warn that short-term 'downward pressure' remains dominant, with traders focusing on the defense of the $1,800–$1,750 support range. CryptoQuant analyst PelinayPA noted that Ethereum's estimated leverage ratio remains relatively high at around 0.74, and funding rates have been positive since April, indicating crowded long positions despite weakening prices. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is approximately 31, nearing oversold territory but not yet signaling a strong rebound.

The U.S. spot Ethereum ETF has recorded net outflows for 13 consecutive trading days, totaling approximately $695 million, with the largest single-day outflow reaching about $121 million. This reflects a continued cooling of institutional demand.

Currently, Ethereum maintains a weak structure amid high leverage, crowded long positions, and ongoing ETF outflows. The $1,800 support level is a crucial point for market sentiment and technical analysis.

data.title: Trump Announces Lifting of Maritime Blockade in Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that the maritime blockade in the Hormuz Strait will be lifted, allowing stranded ships to begin their return journey. According to Odaily, the United States will coordinate with Iran and the International Atomic Energy Agency to excavate and destroy uranium mines. Agreements have been reached on other less critical matters.

data.title: Google Sets Ex-Dividend Date for June 2026
data.description: Alphabet has announced that June 8, 2026, will be the ex-dividend date for Google stockholders to qualify for a quarterly dividend of $0.22. According to NS3.AI, investors need to purchase GOOG shares by the close of trading on June 5, 2026, to be eligible for the dividend. The payment is set to be distributed on June 15, 2026.

data.title: TokenLockr Introduces Smart-Contract System for Influencer Payments
data.description: TokenLockr has unveiled a new smart-contract system designed to tie influencer payments to specific token price performance targets. According to NS3.AI, the company explained that the campaign rewards are divided into two parts: a guaranteed portion and a performance-based portion. This innovative approach aims to align influencer incentives with the success of the token's market performance.

data.title: SoFi Shares Rise 7% on Stablecoin Launch to 15M Users
data.description: Shares of SoFi (SOFI) increased by 7% on Friday following the company's announcement of a new dollar-backed stablecoin, SoFiUSD, which has been rolled out to 15 million users of its banking app. This move positions SoFi as the first U.S. national bank to offer a stablecoin directly to retail customers on a public blockchain, according to CoinDesk. The stock has risen 15% over the past week, trading at $18.01, although it remains 34% lower since the start of the year.

data.title: Quarter of Non-Iranian Oil Tankers Escape Persian Gulf Amid Conflict
data.description: Approximately a quarter of the non-Iranian large oil tankers initially stranded in the Persian Gulf have managed to escape the area amid ongoing conflict. According to Odaily, Bloomberg's compiled shipping data reveals that since the conflict began on February 28, effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz, 109 large vessels, each with a capacity of 700,000 barrels or more, were trapped. Of these, 29 have successfully navigated through this strategic chokepoint. Although the oil and refined products that have exited represent only a small fraction of the total still trapped in the Gulf, they have been quickly absorbed by the market as global inventory buffers shrink at a record pace. Additionally, many ships have turned off their location broadcasting equipment to avoid risks, suggesting the actual number of vessels that have escaped may be higher than reported.

data.title: Grayscale Plans Hyperliquid ETF Stake Sale for $115 Million in HYPE Tokens
data.description:
Grayscale is reportedly in discussions with Hyper Holdings Global LP to sell shares of its proposed Hyperliquid ETF in exchange for approximately 2 million HYPE tokens, valued at around $115 million. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction is intended to serve as seed capital before the fund's listing.

Additionally, Grayscale has renamed the product to 'Grayscale Hyperliquid Staking ETF' and plans to list it on Nasdaq under the ticker HYPG. The inclusion of a staking feature distinguishes it from a standard spot ETF that merely tracks token prices.

data.title: Perpetual Futures Contracts for Cryptocurrencies May Trade on US Platforms
data.description: The main U.S. derivatives regulator has announced that perpetual futures contracts for cryptocurrencies could potentially be traded on registered onshore platforms. According to Bloomberg, this development is contingent upon meeting specific conditions. This move could introduce a popular trading instrument, which has predominantly been available overseas, to the American market.

data.title: Paul Brigner: Zcash Not Banned in the European Union
data.description: Paul Brigner has clarified that Zcash is not banned in the European Union. According to NS3.AI, he explained that EU regulations limit regulated crypto service providers from dealing with accounts and transactions that cannot be verified.

data.title: Trump Administration's Plan to Repaint Eisenhower Office Building Raises Concerns
data.description: The Trump administration has proposed a plan to repaint the Eisenhower Executive Office Building, located adjacent to the White House, sparking concerns among preservationists and architects. Bloomberg posted on X that newly obtained documents reveal details of the swift-moving "beautification" project. The initiative aims to enhance the aesthetic appeal of the historic building, but experts worry about potential impacts on its architectural integrity. The Eisenhower Executive Office Building, known for its distinctive design, has been a subject of preservation efforts over the years. The administration's proposal has prompted discussions about balancing modernization with historical preservation. As the project progresses, stakeholders continue to debate the best approach to maintain the building's historical significance while accommodating contemporary needs.

data.title: Iranian Military Advisor Discusses Ending U.S. Maritime Blockade
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader's military advisor, Mohsen Rezaei, stated in an interview that Iran intends to compel the United States to end its maritime blockade. According to Odaily, Rezaei mentioned that this could be achieved through negotiations, but if met with resistance, Iran would consider direct action. He also expressed optimism about the future of Iran's economy, despite current pressures.

data.title: Apollo and Blackstone Seek Investors for $36 Billion AI Infrastructure Financing
data.description: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone Group are engaging additional investors for a $36 billion debt financing deal aimed at supporting Anthropic's AI infrastructure development. According to Odaily, insiders revealed that the financing will be used to purchase Google's custom TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) chips, which Anthropic will lease. Broadcom, which assisted Google in developing these chips, will guarantee a significant portion of the transaction. This deal is poised to be one of the largest private credit transactions ever and potentially the biggest chip financing debt deal to date.

data.title: STOCKS | Palantir Shares Reach Seven-Week High with 7.3% Increase
data.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR.N) shares have surged to a seven-week high, currently up by 7.3%. According to Jin10, this marks a significant increase in the company's stock performance.

data.title: XLM Surges Over 50% Following DTCC's Integration Announcement
data.description: XLM experienced a significant rally, climbing over 50% this week after the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) revealed plans to integrate its tokenized securities platform with the Stellar Network. According to NS3.AI, this development has sparked increased interest in XLM, leading to notable market movements. CoinGlass data indicates that since May 28, Stellar short liquidations have amounted to $12.41 million, in contrast to $6.82 million in long liquidations. On Friday, XLM reached a trading high of $0.224, marking its highest level since January.
